content: Bottom lines should be 3-5 sentences with specific diligence items

Professional IC memo tone throughout all AI outputs. No marketing language, no superlatives ("excellent", "outstanding"), no hedging without data ("may potentially"). Use precise financial language: "covenant headroom of 2.3pp" not "comfortable margin". Asset class-specific terminology: receivables factoring (Klaim), POS lending (SILQ), BNPL (Tamara), trade credit (Aajil), RNPL (Ejari).

Data gaps must be explicitly flagged in AI outputs, never silently ignored. When a compute function fails or returns unavailable data, append to a DATA GAPS section in the AI context. The AI must caveat its findings based on what data is missing. A visible "could not compute PAR" is infinitely better than a confident "portfolio looks good" that omits PAR. This is the single most dangerous pattern on a financial platform.

Confidence grading on all derived metrics: A (observed - directly from tape column), B (inferred - calculated from tape columns using documented formula), C (derived - estimated from empirical patterns or proxy data). Grade displayed as badge on KPI cards. Example: direct DPD from Expected Collection Days column = grade A; shortfall-based estimated DPD = grade B; Option C empirical benchmark DPD = grade C.
